audience.description: Entry-level network technicians and support professionals who need vendor-neutral N10-009 knowledge plus repeated practical design, command, capture, security, operations, and troubleshooting experience.
entryKnowledge: Operate a computer, terminal, browser, and basic file system; nine to twelve months of networking exposure is recommended but not required.
deviceConstraints: Desktop or tablet browser; optional packet-capture and virtual network lab tools
accessibilityAssumptions: Learners may use keyboard navigation, zoom, screen readers, voice input, or reduced motion; diagrams require equivalent structured text.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Operate a computer, terminal, browser, and basic file system; nine to twelve months of networking exposure is recommended but not required.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 4 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: All CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 objectives and domain weights
Vendor-neutral performance-based configuration, packet, design, security, and troubleshooting practice
Four cumulative branch, wireless, operations, and certification projects
excludes: Live unauthorized scanning or production changes

sources.title: CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 Exam Objectives
sources.scope: Controls every certification domain, objective, acronym, tool, protocol, and performance-based scope decision.

sources.title: Internet Protocol Version 6 Specification
sources.scope: Controls IPv6 packet, addressing, extension, and transition explanations at foundational depth.

sources.title: Zero Trust Architecture
sources.scope: Controls zero-trust terminology, policy decision, trust, identity, and least-privilege coverage.

sources.title: IEEE 802.3 Ethernet Standard
sources.scope: Controls Ethernet media, link, speed, duplex, and physical-layer terminology.
